title: Minimize the admission of HostPath volumes

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of HostPath volumes
input_row.description: Do not generally admit containers which make use of hostPath volumes.
input_row.rationale: A container which mounts a hostPath volume as part of its specification will have access to the filesystem of the underlying cluster node. The use of hostPath volumes may allow containers access to privileged areas of the node filesystem. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to mount hostPath volumes. If you need to run containers which require hostPath volumes, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined which make use of hostPath volumes will not be permitted unless they are run under a spefific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of containers with hostPath volumes.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of containers which use hostPath volumes. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostPath volumes. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.impact: Pods defined which make use of hostPath volumes will not be permitted unless they are run under a spefific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_hostpath_volumes_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster.
- For each namespace, check for the presence and enforcement mode of Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels (pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce).
- If PSA is enabled, verify that the enforced Pod Security Standard (PSS) level is at least "baseline" or "restricted" (these levels disallow hostPath volumes except for specific, tightly-scoped exceptions).
- For clusters using PodSecurityPolicies (PSP, deprecated), enumerate all PSPs and check that those applied to user workloads have spec.volumes not including "hostPath" or have spec.allowedHostPaths set to a minimal, restrictive list.
- For clusters using third-party admission controllers (e.g., OPA/Gatekeeper, Kyverno), enumerate policies and verify that there is at least one policy per namespace (or cluster-wide) that denies pods with hostPath volumes, except for explicitly allowed cases.
- Use the following Kubernetes API/kubectl commands:
  - `kubectl get ns --show-labels` to check PSA labels.
  - `kubectl get psp -o yaml` (if PSPs are enabled).
  - For OPA/Kyverno: `kubectl get constraints` or `kubectl get clusterpolicy` as appropriate.
  - For each namespace, attempt to create a pod spec with a hostPath volume and confirm it is denied (optional, for enforcement validation).
- Pass Criteria: Every namespace with user workloads must have enforced policies (via PSA, PSP, or admission controller) that deny pods with hostPath volumes, except for narrowly-scoped, explicitly-allowed cases.
- Fail Criteria: Any namespace lacking such enforcement, or with policies that allow unrestricted hostPath volumes.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
